BIOLOGY

A Short Story

A young student once wondered why people get sick, how wounds heal, and why plants grow toward sunlight. Those simple questions led the student into the world of Biology. The deeper the student learned, the more they realized that life itself is a fascinating system full of discoveries waiting to be understood.


What Is Biology?

Biology is the study of living organisms and life processes. It explores humans, animals, plants, microorganisms, and the interactions between living things and their environment.


What Will You Study In SHS?

As an SHS student, you may study topics such as:

  • Cell Structure and Function
  • Nutrition
  • Respiration
  • Photosynthesis
  • Genetics and Heredity
  • Human Body Systems
  • Ecology
  • Reproduction
  • Classification of Living Organisms
  • Evolution and Adaptation

Career Opportunities

Biology can lead to careers such as:

  • Medical Doctor
  • Nurse
  • Pharmacist
  • Biomedical Scientist
  • Veterinary Doctor
  • Nutritionist
  • Environmental Scientist
  • Public Health Specialist
  • Research Scientist
  • Lecturer

Something Most Students Don't Know

Many students think Biology is only for people who want to become doctors. In reality, Biology opens doors to healthcare, biotechnology, environmental management, agriculture, public health, genetics, research, and many emerging scientific fields. The future of medicine and biotechnology depends heavily on Biology.


Want To Learn More?

This is only a small introduction. If you want to understand how programmes connect to university courses, careers, opportunities, and future success, get a copy of:
